result.text: Normally, you need a diamond pickaxe to mine obsidian. If you prefer to mine obsidian directly, you'll need to find diamonds, which are usually found deep underground near lava pools between Y-levels 5 and 16.
Surface lava pools are ideal because they often have multiple lava source blocks.
The smallest Nether Portal frame requires 10 obsidian blocks; the corners can be omitted.
Following these steps will get you to the Nether as quickly as possible in a new Minecraft world.

result.text: The first tier ore is Cobalt or Palladium.

result.text: You have 5 days (lives) to escape.
Granny has excellent hearing—dropping items will attract her attention.

If Granny catches you on the fifth day, it’s game over.
If you want a speedrun route, you can use item spawn logic and knockout timing to beat the game in under 5 minutes.

result.text: Use high-heal items like Butterscotch Pie in safe windows when your HP is dangerously low.
Avoid wasting small healing items unless you're confident you'll survive the next attack.
Offense only matters in the brief windows where you can land hits; survival is the main challenge.
Turn off distractions and use headphones to catch audio cues.
Karma damage is a lingering red effect that chips away at your HP after being hit.
Practice in segments: focus on learning one attack pattern at a time if you keep dying at a particular part.
Use audio cues, especially for Gaster Blaster charges, to anticipate attacks.
Minimal movement is safer; overreacting leads to more deaths.
Don’t heal frivolously; save healing items for when you are at real risk of being one-shot.
Expect to reset many times; each attempt helps you learn the patterns.
Stay still during blue, move during white.
Learn the timing of Gaster Blasters and bone wave overlaps.
Use healing items only in the smallest safe windows.
Memorize and isolate patterns—build up the fight mentally in chunks.
Patience beats aggression; survival is the limiter, not raw damage.
If you want, you can use a practice drill regimen, visual cheat sheet, or dodging trainer to help build muscle memory.

result.result: You learn how to read the Junimo language.

result.action: Access the bundles in the Community Center.
result.result: You can now view and begin completing bundles in each room.

result.action: Collect and donate the required items for each bundle in the Crafts Room (Spring Foraging, Summer Foraging, Fall Foraging, Winter Foraging, Construction, Exotic Foraging).
result.result: Completing all bundles in the Crafts Room restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Collect and donate the required items for each bundle in the Pantry (Spring Crops, Summer Crops, Fall Crops, Quality Crops, Animal, Artisan).
result.result: Completing all bundles in the Pantry restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Collect and donate the required items for each bundle in the Fish Tank (River Fish, Lake Fish, Ocean Fish, Night Fishing, Crab Pot, Specialty Fish).
result.result: Completing all bundles in the Fish Tank restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Collect and donate the required items for each bundle in the Boiler Room (Blacksmith's, Geologist's, Adventurer's).
result.result: Completing all bundles in the Boiler Room restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Collect and donate the required items for each bundle on the Bulletin Board (Chef’s, Dye, Field Research, Fodder, Enchanter’s).
result.result: Completing all bundles on the Bulletin Board restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Pay the required gold amounts (2,500g, 5,000g, 10,000g, 25,000g) to complete the Vault bundles.
result.result: Completing all Vault bundles unlocks the Bus Repair and restores that part of the Community Center.

result.action: Complete all bundles in every room of the Community Center.
result.result: The Junimos restore the Community Center to its former glory.

result.action: Wait until the following day after completing all bundles.
result.result: A cutscene shows Pelican Town celebrating the restoration, and you complete one of the major goals of Stardew Valley.

result.text: By completing the Community Center, you prevent the development of the JojaMart Warehouse, preserving the town's charm.
Enjoy the benefits and rewards from each bundle completion, as they will aid you in your farming adventure!

result.action: Familiarize yourself with your cards and their elixir costs.
result.result: You understand the resources required to play each card.

result.action: Learn the strengths and weaknesses of each card and how they complement each other.
result.result: You know how to use your cards effectively in different situations.

result.action: Ensure you always have enough elixir to defend against enemy pushes.
result.result: You are prepared to defend when your opponent attacks.

result.action: Trade elixir positively by spending less elixir on defense than your opponent spends on offense.
result.result: You maintain an elixir advantage over your opponent.

result.action: Include a mix of offensive and defensive cards in your deck.
result.result: Your deck can handle both attacking and defending.

result.action: Ensure your deck has cards to counter various threats such as air and ground units, swarms, and tanks.
result.result: You are prepared to deal with different types of enemy cards.

result.action: Begin the match by playing a low-risk card like a cycle card or a defensive building if available.
result.result: You start the match safely without overcommitting.

result.action: Wait to see what your opponent plays before committing more elixir.
result.result: You avoid overcommitting at the start and can react to your opponent's strategy.

result.action: Support your troops with additional cards to create a strong push.
result.result: You build a powerful offensive push.

result.action: After defending against an attack, use your surviving troops for a counter-push.
result.result: You maximize the value of your defending troops by turning defense into offense.

result.action: Learn the best placement for each card, such as placing ranged attackers behind your towers for defense.
result.result: Your cards are positioned optimally for their roles.

result.action: Time your card deployment to maximize their effectiveness, such as placing splash damage units to counter swarm troops.
result.result: Your cards are used at the most effective moments.

result.action: Use buildings to pull enemy tanks away from your towers.
result.result: Enemy tanks are diverted from your towers, reducing damage taken.

result.action: Use splash damage troops against swarms.
result.result: Enemy swarm units are eliminated efficiently.

result.action: Deploy troops to distract and eliminate enemy units effectively.
result.result: Enemy units are neutralized with minimal damage to your towers.

result.action: Combine tank units with damage-dealing troops for a balanced push.
result.result: You create a push that is difficult for your opponent to stop.

result.action: Utilize spells to support your push by taking out defending troops or weakening towers.
result.result: Your push is strengthened and enemy defenses are weakened.

result.action: Pay attention to your opponent’s card cycle and try to predict their next moves.
result.result: You can anticipate and counter your opponent’s strategies.

result.action: Keep track of what cards your opponent has used and what they might be saving for a big push.
result.result: You are prepared for your opponent’s potential strategies.

result.action: Be flexible and ready to adjust your strategy based on how the match is going and your opponent's play style.
result.result: You can adapt to changing situations during the match.

result.action: If behind, focus on strong defense and chip damage.
result.result: You minimize losses and look for opportunities to catch up.

result.action: If ahead, maintain pressure without overcommitting.
result.result: You keep your advantage while avoiding unnecessary risks.

result.action: In the final minute, recognize that elixir generation doubles, allowing for bigger pushes.
result.result: You can deploy more troops and spells quickly.

result.action: Be prepared for your opponent's final push and have a counter strategy ready.
result.result: You are ready to defend against strong late-game attacks.

result.action: Watch replays of your matches to learn from mistakes and successes.
result.result: You identify areas for improvement in your gameplay.

result.action: Study popular strategies and decks used by top players.
result.result: You gain new insights and ideas to enhance your own strategies.

result.text: By developing these strategies and tactics, you can improve your decision-making and gameplay in Clash Royale.
Practice and experience are key to mastering the game.

result.action: Divide players into two teams.
result.result: Each team is formed with at least one Spymaster and one Field Operative.

result.action: Shuffle the grid of 25 codename cards.
result.result: The codename cards are randomized for the game.

result.action: Place the 25 codename cards in a 5x5 layout on the table.
result.result: The game board is set up for play.

result.action: Spymasters draw a key card.
result.result: The key card shows which codenames belong to which team, which are neutral, and which is the assassin.

result.action: Identify the starting team by checking the border color on the key card.
result.result: The starting team is determined and knows they have an extra agent to find.

result.action: The Spymaster of the starting team provides a one-word clue and a number.
result.result: Field Operatives receive a clue related to their team's codenames and know how many words are related.

result.action: Field Operatives discuss and choose a codename they think matches the clue.
result.result: A codename is selected as a guess.

result.action: Spymaster reveals whether the chosen codename is correct by placing a card over it.
result.result: The codename is covered with the appropriate card: team color, neutral, opposing team, or assassin.

result.action: If the codename is their own team's agent, cover it with their team’s color card.
result.result: The team’s agent is identified on the board.

result.action: If the codename is neutral, cover it with a neutral color card.
result.result: A neutral codename is identified on the board.

result.action: If the codename is the opposing team’s agent, cover it with the opposing team’s color card.
result.result: The opposing team’s agent is identified on the board.

result.action: If the codename is the assassin, cover it with the assassin card.
result.result: The team loses immediately.

result.action: Continue guessing until the number of correct guesses equals the number given by the Spymaster, the team chooses to stop, or they guess incorrectly.
result.result: The team's turn ends.

result.action: Switch turns to the other team.
result.result: The other team begins their turn following the same process.

result.action: Repeat steps 6-14 until a team has identified all their agents or the assassin is guessed.
result.result: The game progresses toward completion.

result.action: If a team correctly identifies all their agents, declare them the winner.
result.result: The winning team is determined.

result.action: If a team guesses the assassin, end the game immediately.
result.result: The team that guessed the assassin loses.

result.text: The clue must relate to the words on the board without using any form of the word itself.
The Spymaster should avoid giving clues that are too direct or breaking the one-word rule.
Field Operatives can make an additional guess beyond the number given by the Spymaster if they want.

result.action: Get incarcerated by committing crimes or failing at life choices.
result.result: You are sent to prison and can attempt an escape.

result.action: Tap on the 'Prison' tab and select 'Escape' to access the escape mini-game.
result.result: The escape mini-game grid is displayed.

result.action: Observe the grid layout, including walls, your character, the guard, and the exit.
result.result: You understand the positions and obstacles in the mini-game.

result.action: Plan your moves, keeping in mind that the guard moves twice for every move you make, prioritizing horizontal movement.
result.result: You anticipate the guard’s movement pattern.

result.action: Avoid moving onto the guard’s tile or letting the guard move onto yours.
result.result: You prevent being caught by the guard.

result.action: Use walls to block the guard’s path or force the guard into a loop.
result.result: You create obstacles for the guard, increasing your chances of escape.

result.action: Think several moves ahead to predict the guard’s path and create situations where the guard is stuck or moves back and forth.
result.result: You gain a strategic advantage over the guard.

result.action: Position yourself to lead the guard away from the exit by using walls and obstacles.
result.result: The guard is diverted, giving you a clearer path to the exit.

result.action: Gradually move closer to the exit while avoiding the guard.
result.result: You approach the exit safely.

result.action: Move to the exit door when you have a clear path.
result.result: You successfully escape from prison.

result.action: After escaping, avoid committing new crimes or being recognized.
result.result: You remain free and avoid being sent back to prison.

result.text: The escape mini-game consists of a grid with walls, the prisoner (you), and a guard.
Your goal is to reach the exit door without being caught by the guard.
The guard always moves twice for every move you make, prioritizing horizontal movement first.
If you or the guard move onto each other's tile, you will be caught.
Take your time with each move and observe the guard’s pattern.
The more you play, the better you’ll become at predicting the guard’s movement.
Don’t be afraid to get caught and try again; each grid can be different.
Use the undo button to rethink your last move if available.
Each prison has a different difficulty level, so strategies may vary depending on the grid.

result.action: Launch 'There Is No Game.'
result.result: You are greeted with a message telling you there's no game.

result.action: Ignore the message and start clicking around.
result.result: You interact with the screen despite the narrator's insistence.

result.action: Click on the letters in the title screen.
result.result: The narrator continues to insist there is no game.

result.action: Focus on the 'O' in 'NO' and click it multiple times.
result.result: The 'O' falls from the title, causing the narrator to react.

result.action: Click and drag the fallen 'O' to hit other letters.
result.result: Other letters fall from the title.

result.action: Continue breaking the letters using the 'O.'
result.result: A secret compartment is revealed.

result.action: Click to open the secret compartment.
result.result: You find a trophy inside.

result.action: Click on the trophy to interact with it.
result.result: The narrator becomes further angered.

result.action: Wait for the narrator to put a block in the way to fix the title.
result.result: A block appears, blocking your progress.

result.action: Drag and drop the trophy onto the block.
result.result: The block breaks.

result.action: Proceed to the new scene with a goat and a locked cage.
result.result: You see a goat in a locked cage.

result.action: Look for the key, which is with a squirrel.
result.result: You discover the key is held by a squirrel.

result.action: Find a way to get a nut to trade with the squirrel.
result.result: You realize you need a nut to trade for the key.

result.action: Use the trophy to knock down the tree.
result.result: A nut falls from the tree.

result.action: Drag the nut to a stone or hard surface.
result.result: The nut cracks open.

result.action: Drag the cracked nut to the squirrel.
result.result: The squirrel gives you the key in exchange for the nut.

result.action: Use the key on the cage.
result.result: The goat is freed.

result.action: Watch the series of humorous events that unfold.
result.result: The game moves toward its conclusion.

result.action: Listen to the narrator's final commentary.
result.result: You receive final messages from the narrator.

result.action: Finish the game.
result.result: You have completed 'There Is No Game.'

result.text: The charm of 'There Is No Game' lies in its humor and unexpected twists, so enjoy the playful interaction with the narrator and the amusing scenarios.

result.action: Turn on your PS5.
result.result: The PS5 powers on and is ready for use.

result.action: Sign in to the account that you want to set as primary.
result.result: You are signed in to the desired PlayStation account.

result.action: Navigate to the top right corner of the main PS5 dashboard and click on the 'Settings' icon (gear symbol).
result.result: The Settings menu opens.

result.action: In the Settings menu, scroll down and select 'Users and Accounts.'
result.result: The Users and Accounts menu is displayed.

result.action: Within the Users and Accounts menu, scroll down and select 'Other.'
result.result: The 'Other' submenu is opened.

result.action: Choose 'Console Sharing and Offline Play.'
result.result: The Console Sharing and Offline Play settings are displayed.

result.action: If it’s not already enabled, select 'Enable' for Console Sharing and Offline Play.
result.result: Your PS5 is set as the primary console for that account.

result.action: Ensure that the games you want to share are downloaded and installed on your PS5.
result.result: The desired games are available on your PS5 for sharing.

result.action: Other users can create a new account or sign in with their existing account on the same PS5 by going to the top right corner, selecting 'Profile,' then 'Switch User,' and choosing 'Add User.'
result.result: Additional users are added to the PS5.

result.action: Have other users access the PS5 with their accounts.
result.result: Other users can access and play shared digital games on the same console.

result.action: Play digital games even when not connected to the internet with Console Sharing and Offline Play enabled.
result.result: You can play digital games offline.

result.text: If you have reached the limit of activated primary consoles, you might need to deactivate the previous primary console. You can do this from the PS5 by going to 'Settings' > 'Users and Accounts' > 'Other' > 'Console Sharing and Offline Play' and choosing 'Disable' on the other console.
Console Sharing and Offline Play can only be enabled on one PS5 at a time per account.

result.action: Ensure your character is at least 18 years old.
result.result: Your character is eligible to commit crimes like train robberies.

result.action: Go to the 'Activities' tab in BitLife.
result.result: You access the list of available activities.

result.action: Select 'Crime' from the list of activities.
result.result: You open the crime menu.

result.action: Find and click on the 'Train Robbery' option.
result.result: You begin planning the train robbery.

result.action: Improve your character’s Smarts and Craziness stats before attempting the robbery.
result.result: Your chances of a successful robbery increase.

result.action: Choose your timing and approach for the robbery based on the options provided.
result.result: You select the method and timing for the train robbery.

result.action: Proceed with executing the train robbery.
result.result: The outcome of the robbery is determined by your choices and character’s stats.

result.action: Deal with the consequences of the robbery.
result.result: If successful, you receive loot; if you fail, you may be caught and sent to prison.

result.action: Attempt to rob more trains in future years if desired.
result.result: You can continue to experience train robberies and refine your strategy.

result.text: Outcomes in BitLife are unpredictable, and there is always a risk of getting caught.
Experimenting with different strategies can enhance your enjoyment of the game.

result.action: Power on the Steam Deck.
result.result: The Steam Deck is turned on and you are logged into your account.

result.action: Press the Steam button on the left side of the device.
result.result: The main menu opens.

result.action: Scroll down and select 'Settings' in the menu.
result.result: The Settings menu opens on the right side of the screen.

result.action: Scroll down and select 'Display' within the Settings menu.
result.result: The Display section opens, allowing you to adjust various display settings.

result.action: Look for the 'Resolution' settings in the Display section.
result.result: You see the current resolution setting.

result.action: Select the Resolution option.
result.result: A list of available resolutions appears.

result.action: Choose your desired resolution from the list.
result.result: The desired resolution is selected (available options may vary depending on the display in use).

result.action: Confirm the change if prompted by selecting 'Apply', or wait for the system to automatically apply the new resolution.
result.result: The new resolution is set. If not confirmed within a certain time frame, the system may revert to the previous resolution.

result.action: Press the 'B' button or the 'Steam' button again to exit the settings menu.
result.result: You return to your previous activity.

result.text: The Steam Deck's native display resolution is 1280x800. Adjusting to a higher resolution is typically only possible when using an external display.
Changing resolution settings may impact performance, especially in graphically demanding games.
If you encounter any display issues, you can always return to the Display settings and revert to the original resolution.
